entries.name: Prompt Injection
entries.description: Attackers manipulate LLM behaviour by crafting inputs that override system instructions or cause unintended actions. Can be direct (via user input) or indirect (via external content the LLM retrieves).

entries.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
entries.description: LLMs may expose sensitive data from training, context windows, or retrieved documents through normal interactions or adversarial prompting.

entries.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
entries.description: Risks introduced through third-party model providers, datasets, plugins, or fine-tuned models that are outside the organisation's direct control.

entries.name: Data and Model Poisoning
entries.description: Malicious manipulation of training data or fine-tuning processes to introduce backdoors, biases, or vulnerabilities into the model.

entries.name: Insecure Output Handling
entries.description: Downstream components consume LLM output without sufficient validation, enabling injection attacks such as XSS, SQLi, or command injection via model responses.

entries.name: Excessive Agency
entries.description: LLM-based agents are granted more permissions, capabilities, or autonomy than required, enabling unintended or harmful real-world actions.

entries.name: System Prompt Leakage
entries.description: System prompts containing sensitive instructions, credentials, or business logic are exposed through adversarial prompting or verbose error responses.

entries.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
entries.description: Vulnerabilities in RAG pipelines including poisoned vector stores, embedding inversion attacks, and insufficient access controls on retrieved documents.

entries.name: Misinformation
entries.description: LLMs generate plausible but incorrect information that downstream systems or users act on without verification, leading to harmful decisions.

entries.name: Unbounded Consumption
entries.description: Uncontrolled resource usage by LLM workloads enables denial of service, runaway costs, or side-channel attacks through inference timing.
